SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS = {".pdf", ".docx", ".pptx"} 10: 11: 12: def detect_file_type(filename: str) -> str | None: 13: ext = Path(filename).suffix.lower() 14: if ext == ".pdf": 15: return "pdf" 16: elif ext == ".docx": 17: return "docx" 18: elif ext == ".pptx": 19: return "pptx" 20: return None 21: 22: 23: def ocr_image_file(file_path: str) -> str: 24: with open(file_path, "rb") as f: 25: return ocr_image_bytes(f.read()) 26: 27: 28: def convert_to_pdf(input_path: str, output_dir: str) -> str: 29: import comtypes.client 30: import pythoncom 31: 32: pythoncom.CoInitialize() 33: 34: src = Path(input_path).resolve() 35: out = Path(output_dir).resolve() / f"{src.stem}.pdf" 36: ext = src.suffix.lower() 37: 38: try: 39: if ext == ".docx": 40: app = comtypes.client.CreateObject("Word.Application") 41: app.Visible = False 42: doc = app.Documents.Open(str(src)) 43: doc.ExportAsFixedFormat(str(out), 17) 44: doc.Close() 45: app.Quit() 46: 47: elif ext == ".pptx": 48: app = comtypes.client.CreateObject("PowerPoint.Application") 49: app.Visible = False 50: pres = app.Presentations.Open(str(src)) 51: pres.SaveAs(str(out), 32) 52: pres.Close() 53: app.Quit() 54: 55: else: 56: raise ValueError(f"Unsupported for COM conversion: {ext}") 57: 58: finally: 59: pythoncom.CoUninitialize() 60: 61: return str(out) 62: 63: 64: def _extract_pdf(path: str) -> list[dict]: 65: try: 66: text = ocr_pdf_file(path) 67: if text.strip(): 68: return [{"page": 1, "source": "gemini", "text": text}] 69: except Exception: 70: pass 71: import fitz 72: doc = fitz.open(path) 73: try: 74: pages = [] 75: for page_num, page in enumerate(doc): 76: native_text = page.get_text().strip() 77: pix = page.get_pixmap(dpi=200) 78: img_bytes = pix.tobytes("png") 79: ocr_text = _ocr_image_bytes(img_bytes) 80: combined = f"{native_text}
{ocr_text}".strip() 81: source = "native+ocr" if native_text and ocr_text else ("ocr" if ocr_text else "native") 82: pages.append({ 83: "page": page_num + 1, 84: "source": source, 85: "text": combined, 86: }) 87: return pages 88: finally: 89: doc.close() 90: 91: 92: def _try_gemini_pdf(path: str) -> str | None: 93: with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir: 94: try: 95: pdf_path = convert_to_pdf(path, tmpdir) 96: text = ocr_pdf_file(pdf_path) 97: if text.strip(): 98: return text 99: except Exception: 100: pass 101: return None 102: 103: 104: def _extract_docx(path: str) -> list[dict]: 105: text = _try_gemini_pdf(path) 106: if text is not None: 107: return [{"page": 1, "source": "gemini", "text": text}] 108: from docx import Document 109: doc = Document(path) 110: paragraphs = [p.text for p in doc.paragraphs] 111: native_text = "
".join(paragraphs).strip() 112: pages = [{"page": 1, "source": "native", "text": native_text}] 113: ocr_parts = [] 114: for rel in doc.part.rels.values(): 115: if "image" in rel.reltype: 116: image_data = rel.target_part.blob 117: text = _ocr_image_bytes(image_data) 118: if text.strip(): 119: ocr_parts.append(text) 120: if ocr_parts: 121: pages.append({"page": 1, "source": "native+ocr", "text": "
".join(ocr_parts)}) 122: return pages 123: 124: 125: def _extract_pptx(path: str) -> list[dict]: 126: text = _try_gemini_pdf(path) 127: if text is not None: 128: return [{"page": 1, "source": "gemini", "text": text}] 129: from pptx import Presentation 130: from pptx.enum.shapes import MSO_SHAPE_TYPE 131: prs = Presentation(path) 132: slides_data = [] 133: for slide_num, slide in enumerate(prs.slides): 134: texts = [] 135: for shape in slide.shapes: 136: if shape.has_text_frame and shape.text_frame.text.strip(): 137: texts.append(shape.text_frame.text) 138: if shape.shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.PICTURE: 139: img_data = shape.image.blob 140: ocr_text = _ocr_image_bytes(img_data) 141: if ocr_text.strip(): 142: texts.append(ocr_text) 143: slides_data.append({ 144: "page": slide_num + 1, 145: "source": "native", 146: "text": "
".join(texts), 147: }) 148: return slides_data 149: 150: 151: def process_document( 152: file_path: str, 153: chat_id: int, 154: message_id: int, 155: sender_name: str, 156: sent_at: datetime, 157: chat_title: str | None = None, 158: ) -> dict: 159: path = Path(file_path) 160: file_type = detect_file_type(path.name) 161: 162: if file_type == "pdf": 163: pages = _extract_pdf(file_path) 164: elif file_type == "docx": 165: pages = _extract_docx(file_path) 166: elif file_type == "pptx": 167: pages = _extract_pptx(file_path) 168: else: 169: raise ValueError(f"Unsupported file type: {file_type}") 170: 171: full_text = "

After the call it 8: prints the in-memory DAG state so you can see exactly what moved. 9: 10: Usage: 11: python -m backend.tests.sim_message "T03 cabling done" 12: python -m backend.tests.sim_message "material missing at T04" --telegram-id 12345 13: 14: Requires the same .env (SUPABASE_URL/KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY) the bot uses, since 15: the real LLM + real Supabase run here. It WILL write to original_messages / 16: processed_messages / tasks / task_events, just like a real message. 17: """ 18: import argparse 19: 20: from backend.integrations.supabase.client import get_supabase_client 21: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import SyncFieldDAG 22: from backend.optimization.vrp_solver.solver import VRPSolver 23: from backend.integrations.notifications.stub import StubNotifier 24: from backend.workflow.event_handlers.dispatcher import FieldOpsDispatcher 25: from backend.llm.pipeline import process_message, set_dispatcher 26: 27: 28: def build_dispatcher(): 29: """Same dependency order as bot.py main(), minus Telethon + Realtime.""" 30: sb = get_supabase_client() 31: 32: db_tasks = sb.table("tasks").select("*").execute().data or [] 33: for t in db_tasks: 34: t.setdefault("task_id", t.pop("id", None)) 35: engine = SyncFieldDAG(db_tasks) 36: 37: tech_data = sb.table("technicians").select("*").execute().data or [] 38: tech_db = {t["id"]: t for t in tech_data} 39: 40: solver = VRPSolver(engine, tech_db) 41: dispatcher = FieldOpsDispatcher(engine, solver, StubNotifier(), sb_client=sb) 42: set_dispatcher(dispatcher) 43: return dispatcher 44: 45: 46: def main(): 47: ap = argparse.ArgumentParser() 48: ap.add_argument("text", help="the message text, as if typed in Telegram") 49: ap.add_argument("--telegram-id", default=None, help="sender telegram id") 50: args = ap.parse_args() 51: 52: dispatcher = build_dispatcher() 53: engine = dispatcher.engine 54: 55: print("
===== DAG BEFORE =====") 56: engine.print_state() 57: 58: message = { 59: "sender_name": "sim", 60: "sent_at": None, 61: "chat_title": "sim", 62: "type": "text", 63: "text": args.text, 64: } 65: result = process_message(message, telegram_id=args.telegram_id, dispatcher=dispatcher) 66: 67: print("
===== LLM RESULT =====") 68: print(result) 69: 70: print("
===== DAG AFTER =====") 71: engine.print_state() 72: 73: 74: if __name__ == "__main__": 75: main() 1: """ 2: Test the SupabaseEventBus idempotency guard and cascade logic 3: with a mocked DAG engine and fake payloads. 4: 5: Run: PYTHONPATH=. python -m backend.tests.test_event_bus 6: """ 7: from unittest.mock import MagicMock 8: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import SyncFieldDAG 9: from backend.workflow.event_handlers.event_bus import SupabaseEventBus 10: 11: 12: class FakeDispatcher: 13: def __init__(self, engine): 14: self.engine = engine 15: 16: 17: def test_idempotency_guard_skips_matching_state(): 18: """ 19: If memory state == db state, the bus should NOT call update_task_state. 20: This is the infinite-loop prevention guard. 21: """ 22: engine = SyncFieldDAG([ 23: {"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "Test", "dependencies": []}, 24: ]) 25: engine.tasks["T01"]["state"] = "READY" 26: 27: bus = SupabaseEventBus(FakeDispatcher(engine), MagicMock()) 28: original_update = engine.update_task_state 29: call_count = [0] 30: 31: def counting_update(*args, **kwargs): 32: call_count[0] += 1 33: return original_update(*args, **kwargs) 34: 35: engine.update_task_state = counting_update 36: 37: bus.handle_db_change({ 38: "new": {"id": "T01", "state": "READY"} # matches memory 39: }) 40: 41: assert call_count[0] == 0, f"Idempotency guard failed: engine called {call_count[0]} times" 42: print("PASS: Idempotency guard — matching state correctly skipped") 43: 44: 45: def test_processes_genuine_external_change(): 46: """ 47: If memory state != db state, the bus SHOULD call update_task_state. 48: """ 49: engine = SyncFieldDAG([ 50: {"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "Test", "dependencies": []}, 51: {"task_id": "T02", "task_name": "Child", "dependencies": ["T01"]}, 52: ]) 53: engine.tasks["T01"]["state"] = "ACTIVE" 54: engine.tasks["T02"]["state"] = "READY" 55: 56: bus = SupabaseEventBus(FakeDispatcher(engine), MagicMock()) 57: call_records = [] 58: 59: def record_call(*args, **kwargs): 60: call_records.append((args, kwargs)) 61: return [] 62: 63: engine.update_task_state = record_call 64: 65: bus.handle_db_change({ 66: "new": {"id": "T01", "state": "BLOCKED"} # different from memory 67: }) 68: 69: assert len(call_records) == 1, f"Expected 1 call, got {len(call_records)}" 70: args, kwargs = call_records[0] 71: assert args[0] == "T01", f"Wrong task_id: {args[0]}" 72: assert args[1] == "BLOCKED", f"Wrong state: {args[1]}" 73: print("PASS: Genuine external change correctly triggers engine") 74: 75: 76: def test_ignores_missing_task(): 77: """ 78: If the task_id doesn't exist in the engine, skip silently. 79: """ 80: engine = SyncFieldDAG([ 81: {"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "Test", "dependencies": []}, 82: ]) 83: call_count = [0] 84: 85: def counting_update(*args, **kwargs): 86: call_count[0] += 1 87: return [] 88: 89: engine.update_task_state = counting_update 90: bus = SupabaseEventBus(FakeDispatcher(engine), MagicMock()) 91: 92: bus.handle_db_change({ 93: "new": {"id": "T99", "state": "BLOCKED"} # doesn't exist 94: }) 95: 96: assert call_count[0] == 0 97: print("PASS: Missing task correctly ignored") 98: 99: 100: def test_ignores_null_payload(): 101: """ 102: Empty payload, null 'new', null state — all should be skipped. 103: """ 104: engine = SyncFieldDAG([ 105: {"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "Test", "dependencies": []}, 106: ]) 107: call_count = [0] 108: engine.update_task_state = lambda *a, **kw: (call_count.__setitem__(0, call_count[0] + 1) or []) 109: bus = SupabaseEventBus(FakeDispatcher(engine), MagicMock()) 110: 111: for payload in [ 112: {}, 113: {"new": None}, 114: {"new": {}}, 115: {"new": {"id": "T01"}}, 116: {"new": {"state": "READY"}}, 117: ]: 118: bus.handle_db_change(payload) 119: 120: assert call_count[0] == 0, f"Expected 0 calls for null payloads, got {call_count[0]}" 121: print("PASS: All null payload variants correctly skipped") 122: 123: 124: if __name__ == "__main__": 125: test_idempotency_guard_skips_matching_state() 126: test_processes_genuine_external_change() 127: test_ignores_missing_task() 128: test_ignores_null_payload() 129: print("
" + "=" * 60) 130: print("ALL EVENT BUS TESTS PASSED") 131: print("=" * 60) 1: """ 2: P0 — Custom FailurePolicy injection. 3: 4: The dispatcher exposes FieldOpsDispatcher(..., policies=custom_policies) as 5: a configuration point, but DEFAULT_POLICIES is the only thing ever exercised. 6: This test builds a CUSTOM policy set (different retry counts, different 7: escalation roles) and verifies the dispatcher actually obeys it. 8: 9: Run: PYTHONPATH=. python -m backend.tests.test_p0_custom_policy 10: """ 11: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import SyncFieldDAG, FailurePolicy 12: from backend.optimization.vrp_solver.solver import VRPSolver 13: from backend.integrations.notifications.stub import StubNotifier 14: from backend.workflow.event_handlers.dispatcher import FieldOpsDispatcher 15: 16: 17: def section(title): 18: print("
" + "=" * 70) 19: print(title) 20: print("=" * 70) 21: 22: 23: def run(): 24: section("P0: Custom FailurePolicy — TECHNICAL retries 3x instead of default 1x") 25: 26: tasks = [{"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "IP Setup", "dependencies": []}] 27: engine = SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 28: engine.tasks["T01"]["assigned_to"] = "Zul_IT_Tech" 29: 30: custom_policies = { 31: "RESOURCE": FailurePolicy("RESOURCE", "procurement_lead", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=8.0), 32: "SITE": FailurePolicy("SITE", "project_manager", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=10.0), 33: "TECHNICAL": FailurePolicy("TECHNICAL", "senior_network_lead", max_local_retries=3, urgency_weight=20.0), 34: "QUALITY": FailurePolicy("QUALITY", "site_supervisor", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=5.0), 35: } 36: 37: solver = VRPSolver(engine, {"Zul_IT_Tech": {"skills": [], "lat": 0, "lng": 0}}) 38: notifier = StubNotifier() 39: dispatcher = FieldOpsDispatcher(engine, solver, notifier, policies=custom_policies) 40: 41: engine.update_task_state("T01", "ACTIVE", "Zul starts config") 42: 43: actions = [] 44: for attempt in range(1, 5): 45: decision = dispatcher.process_failure_report("T01", "IP_CONFLICT", "Zul_IT_Tech") 46: actions.append(decision["action"]) 47: print(f"Attempt {attempt}: action={decision['action']}, " 48: f"escalate_to_role={decision.get('escalate_to_role', 'n/a')}") 49: if decision["action"] == "ESCALATE": 50: break 51: engine.tasks["T01"]["state"] = "ACTIVE" 52: 53: print(f"
Action sequence: {actions}") 54: expected = ["RETRY_LOCAL", "RETRY_LOCAL", "RETRY_LOCAL", "ESCALATE"] 55: retries_ok = actions == expected 56: print(f"
>>> Retry count honored custom policy (expected {expected}): {'PASS' if retries_ok else 'FAIL'}") 57: 58: return {"retries_ok": retries_ok, "actions": actions} 59: 60: 61: def run_role_check(): 62: section("P0 (continued): verify escalation role comes from the CUSTOM policy") 63: 64: tasks = [{"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "IP Setup", "dependencies": []}] 65: engine = SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 66: engine.tasks["T01"]["assigned_to"] = "Zul_IT_Tech" 67: 68: custom_policies = { 69: "RESOURCE": FailurePolicy("RESOURCE", "procurement_lead", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=8.0), 70: "SITE": FailurePolicy("SITE", "project_manager", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=10.0), 71: "TECHNICAL": FailurePolicy("TECHNICAL", "senior_network_lead", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=20.0), 72: "QUALITY": FailurePolicy("QUALITY", "site_supervisor", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=5.0), 73: } 74: 75: solver = VRPSolver(engine, {"Zul_IT_Tech": {"skills": [], "lat": 0, "lng": 0}}) 76: dispatcher = FieldOpsDispatcher(engine, solver, StubNotifier(), policies=custom_policies) 77: 78: engine.update_task_state("T01", "ACTIVE") 79: decision = dispatcher.process_failure_report("T01", "IP_CONFLICT", "Zul_IT_Tech") 80: 81: print(f"Action: {decision['action']}") 82: print(f"Escalated to: {decision.get('escalate_to_role')}") 83: 84: expected_role = "senior_network_lead" 85: role_ok = decision.get("escalate_to_role") == expected_role 86: immediate_escalate_ok = decision["action"] == "ESCALATE" 87: 88: print(f"
>>> Escalation role matches custom policy ({expected_role}): " 89: f"{'PASS' if role_ok else 'FAIL'} (got {decision.get('escalate_to_role')})") 90: print(f">>> max_local_retries=0 caused immediate escalation: " 91: f"{'PASS' if immediate_escalate_ok else 'FAIL'}") 92: 93: return {"role_ok": role_ok, "immediate_escalate_ok": immediate_escalate_ok} 94: 95: 96: if __name__ == "__main__": 97: r1 = run() 98: r2 = run_role_check() 99: print("
" + "#" * 70) 100: all_pass = r1["retries_ok"] and r2["role_ok"] and r2["immediate_escalate_ok"] 101: print(f"P0 CUSTOM FAILURE POLICY: {'ALL PASS' if all_pass else 'FAILURES FOUND'}") 102: print("#" * 70) 1: """ 2: P0 — Reverse cascade: COMPLETE -> REGRESSED propagation through descendants 3: that are themselves already COMPLETE. 4: 5: This is the test the user correctly identified as missing and as the single 6: most important one: every cascade test so far drove failures FORWARD from 7: a node that hadn't finished yet. This test instead completes an ENTIRE 8: multi-layer chain first, then regresses a node in the MIDDLE, and checks 9: that every downstream node that was COMPLETE flips to REGRESSED -- which is 10: the actual claim behind "bidirectional DAG." 11: 12: Run: PYTHONPATH=. python -m backend.tests.test_p0_regression_cascade 13: """ 14: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import SyncFieldDAG 15: 16: 17: def section(title): 18: print("
" + "=" * 70) 19: print(title) 20: print("=" * 70) 21: 22: 23: def build_chain(): 24: tasks = [ 25: {"task_id": "A", "task_name": "Survey", "dependencies": []}, 26: {"task_id": "B", "task_name": "Conduit", "dependencies": ["A"]}, 27: {"task_id": "C", "task_name": "CableRun", "dependencies": ["B"]}, 28: {"task_id": "D", "task_name": "RackSetup", "dependencies": ["B"]}, 29: {"task_id": "E", "task_name": "Termination", "dependencies": ["C", "D"]}, 30: ] 31: return SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 32: 33: 34: def run(): 35: section("P0 TEST 1: Full completion, then regress B mid-chain") 36: 37: engine = build_chain() 38: for t_id in ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E"]: 39: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "ACTIVE") 40: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "COMPLETE") 41: 42: print("
All tasks COMPLETE. States:") 43: for t_id in ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E"]: 44: print(f" {t_id}: {engine.tasks[t_id]['state']}") 45: 46: print("
Now regressing B (e.g. inspection finds the conduit was never actually installed):") 47: events = engine.update_task_state("B", "REGRESSED", "Inspection found conduit missing") 48: 49: print(f"
{len(events)} events generated:") 50: for e in events: 51: print(f" depth={e.get('depth')} | {e['task_id']:<3} | {e['old_state']:<10} -> {e['new_state']:<10} | {e['reason']}") 52: 53: print("
Final states:") 54: failures = [] 55: expected = {"A": "COMPLETE", "B": "REGRESSED", "C": "REGRESSED", "D": "REGRESSED", "E": "REGRESSED"} 56: for t_id, exp in expected.items(): 57: actual = engine.tasks[t_id]["state"] 58: status = "OK" if actual == exp else "FAIL" 59: if status == "FAIL": 60: failures.append(t_id) 61: print(f" {t_id}: {actual} (expected {exp}) [{status}]") 62: 63: if failures: 64: print(f"
>>> P0 FAILURE: nodes {failures} did not regress correctly.") 65: print(">>> This means 'bidirectional DAG' is not actually proven for multi-layer chains.") 66: else: 67: print("
>>> P0 PASS: COMPLETE descendants correctly cascade to REGRESSED, " 68: "multiple layers deep, through a multi-parent join (E depends on both C and D).") 69: 70: return {"failures": failures} 71: 72: 73: def run_partial_regression(): 74: section("P0 TEST 2: Partial regression — only one of two parents regresses") 75: 76: engine = build_chain() 77: for t_id in ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E"]: 78: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "ACTIVE") 79: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "COMPLETE") 80: 81: print("Regressing C only (D stays COMPLETE). E depends on both C AND D.") 82: engine.update_task_state("C", "REGRESSED", "Cable damaged, found during QA") 83: 84: print(f"
D state (should remain COMPLETE, untouched): {engine.tasks['D']['state']}") 85: print(f"E state (should regress, since C is one of its deps): {engine.tasks['E']['state']}") 86: 87: ok = engine.tasks["D"]["state"] == "COMPLETE" and engine.tasks["E"]["state"] == "REGRESSED" 88: print(f"

run `npx prisma migrate dev` for schema changes 35: - Requires `DATABASE_URL` env var (loaded via `dotenv` in `prisma.config.ts`) 36: 37: ## Environment 38: 39: Required vars: `DATABASE_URL`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `BETTER_AUTH_URL` 40: Required (Supabase): `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` (Python), `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (frontend) 41: 42: **`.env` contains live credentials** (Telegram bot token, Google OAuth secrets) — never log or commit them. 43: 44: ## Workflow 45: 46: - **Never push directly to `main`** — always create a PR from a feature branch. 47: 48: ## Caveats 49: 50: - No test framework installed — skip any test commands 51: - No CI pipeline or `.github/` directory 52: - Python/Telegram bot artifacts present (`bot_session.session`, `__pycache__/`) but unrelated to the Next.js app 1: -- Canonical operations contract shared by the Next.js dashboard and Python worker. 2: -- Safe to run repeatedly against the existing Supabase project. 3: 4: alter table projects add column if not exists client text default ''; 54: 55: -- Claim one command transactionally. SKIP LOCKED keeps multiple worker replicas safe. 56: create or replace function claim_next_task_command() 57: returns setof task_commands 58: language plpgsql 59: security definer 60: set search_path = public 61: as $$ 62: declare 63: claimed_id uuid; 64: begin 65: select id into claimed_id 66: from task_commands 67: where status = 'pending' 68: order by created_at 69: for update skip locked 70: limit 1; 71: 72: if claimed_id is null then 73: return; 74: end if; 75: 76: return query 77: update task_commands 78: set status = 'processing', started_at = now(), error = null 79: where id = claimed_id 80: returning *; 81: end; 82: $$; 83: 84: revoke all on function claim_next_task_command() from public, anon, authenticated; 85: grant execute on function claim_next_task_command() to service_role; 86: 87: -- Operational rows are readable only with a short-lived authenticated JWT. 88: -- Writes are performed by authenticated Next.js handlers or the service-role worker. 89: alter table sites enable row level security; Engine should reject this at construction time.") 25: 26: import time 27: start = time.time() 28: try: 29: engine = SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 30: elapsed = time.time() - start 31: print(f"
Unexpectedly SUCCEEDED in {elapsed:.3f}s — silent deadlock accepted.") 32: print(f"T01 state: {engine.tasks['T01']['state']}") 33: print(f"T02 state: {engine.tasks['T02']['state']}") 34: return {"hung": False, "rejected": False} 35: except ValueError as e: 36: elapsed = time.time() - start 37: print(f"
Correctly rejected in {elapsed:.3f}s: {e}") 38: return {"hung": False, "rejected": True} 39: 40: 41: def run_failed_task_reporting_failure(): 42: section("P2 TEST 2: A FAILED (terminal) task receives another failure report") 43: 44: tasks = [{"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "Install", "dependencies": []}] 45: engine = SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 46: engine.tasks["T01"]["assigned_to"] = "Ahmad" 47: 48: engine.update_task_state("T01", "ACTIVE") 49: engine.update_task_state("T01", "BLOCKED", "first failure") 50: engine.update_task_state("T01", "FAILED", "abandoned after no fix possible") 51: print(f"T01 is now FAILED (terminal state).") 52: 53: domain_rules = FieldOpsDomainRules(DEFAULT_POLICIES) 54: 55: print("
Now a technician reports ANOTHER failure against the same (FAILED) task:") 56: decision = domain_rules.handle_task_failure(engine, "T01", "MATERIAL_MISSING", "Ahmad") 57: print(f"Result: action={decision['action']}, msg={decision['msg']}") 58: print(f"T01 state after: {engine.tasks['T01']['state']} (unchanged)") 59: 60: guarded = decision["action"] == "ALREADY_FAILED" and engine.tasks["T01"]["state"] == "FAILED" 61: print(f"
>>> {'PASS' if guarded else 'FAIL'}: ALREADY_FAILED guard correctly no-ops without exception") 62: return {"crashed": False, "guarded": guarded} 63: 64: 65: if __name__ == "__main__": 66: r1 = run_cyclic_dependency() 67: r2 = run_failed_task_reporting_failure() 68: 69: print("

19: const activity = <Card><CardContent><h3 className="font-semibold text-slate-950">Backend activity</h3><div className="mt-4 space-y-3">{snapshot.taskEvents.filter((event) => tasks.some((task) => task.id === event.taskId)).slice(0, 30).map((event) => <div key={event.id} className="rounded-lg border border-slate-100 p-3"><div className="flex items-center gap-2"><Badge value={event.newState.toLowerCase()} /><span className="text-xs font-medium text-slate-800">{tasks.find((task) => task.id === event.taskId)?.title || event.taskId}</span></div><p className="mt-2 text-xs text-slate-500">{event.reason}</p><p className="mt-1 text-[10px] text-slate-400">{new Date(event.createdAt).toLocaleString()} · depth {event.depth}</p></div>)}</div></CardContent></Card>; 20: return <><Link href="/projects" className="mb-4 inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs font-semibold text-slate-500 hover:text-orange-600"><IconArrowLeft className="size-3.5" />Back to projects</Link>{error && <div className="mb-4 rounded-lg border border-red-200 bg-red-50 px-4 py-3 text-sm text-red-700">{error}</div>}<div className="mb-6 rounded-2xl border border-slate-200 bg-white p-5 shadow-sm"><div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3"><h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-950">{project.name}</h2><Badge value={project.status} /></div><p className="mt-2 text-sm text-slate-500">{project.client}</p><div className="mt-4 flex flex-wrap gap-6 text-xs text-slate-500"><span className="flex items-center gap-1.5"><IconMapPin className="size-4" />{project.location}</span><span className="flex items-center gap-1.5"><IconCalendarMonth className="size-4" />{project.startDate || "Unscheduled"} – {project.endDate || "Unscheduled"}</span><span className="flex items-center gap-1.5"><IconUsers className="size-4" />{members.length} assigned</span></div></div><Tabs useHash tabs={[{ value: "overview", label: "Overview", content: overview }, { value: "schedule", label: "DAG Board", content: <KanbanBoard projectId={project.id} /> }, { value: "timeline", label: "Timeline", content: <ProjectDetailTimeline projectId={project.id} /> }, { value: "activity", label: "Activity", content: activity }]} /></>; 48: state text not null default 'LOCKED', 49: dependencies text[] default '{}', 50: assigned_to uuid references technicians(id), 51: failure_category text, 52: attempt_count int default 0, 53: blocked_since timestamptz, 54: scheduled_start timestamptz, 55: deadline timestamptz, 56: required_skills text[], 57: requires_shared_tool boolean default false, 58: created_at timestamptz default now(), 59: updated_at timestamptz default now() 60: ); 61: 62: -- Original messages (raw, exactly what came in from Telegram, before any LLM analysis) 63: create table if not exists original_messages ( 64: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 65: telegram_id text, 66: sender_name text, 67: chat_title text, 68: sent_at timestamptz, 69: type text, 70: text text not null, 71: replied_to text, 72: created_at timestamptz default now() 73: ); 74: 75: -- Processed messages (Tier 1/2/3 resolution audit trail — the LLM's classification of one original_messages row) 76: create table if not exists processed_messages ( 77: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 78: original_message_id uuid references original_messages(id) on delete cascade, 79: technician_id uuid references technicians(id), 80: task_id text references tasks(id), 81: label text, 82: status text, 83: material text, 84: quantity numeric, 85: resolved_state text, 86: resolved_failure_type text, 87: tier_resolved text, 88: confidence numeric, 89: note text, 90: created_at timestamptz default now() 91: ); 92: 93: -- Task events (state transitions — drives cascade animation) 94: create table if not Without this lock a 38: # transition can run on a stale graph before a create's reload lands 39: # ("Task ... not found"). 40: self._lock = threading.Lock() 41: 42: def _ensure_engine_task(self, task_id: str) -> None: 43: """Guarantee the in-memory graph has the task before mutating it. Reloads 44: once from the DB to absorb a just-committed create; raises a clear error 45: only if it genuinely does not exist.""" 46: if task_id in self.dispatcher.engine.tasks: 47: return 48: self.reload_graph() 49: if task_id not in self.dispatcher.engine.tasks: 50: raise ValueError(f"Task {task_id} not found") 51: 52: def _valid_assignee(self, value): 53: """assigned_to is a uuid FK to technicians; drop anything that is not a 54: known technician (dashboard actor ids, 'llm', stale ids).""" 55: return value if value in self.solver.technicians else None 56: 57: def reload_graph(self) -> None: 58: rows = self.sb.table("tasks").select("*").execute().data or [] 59: hydrated = hydrate_task_rows(rows) 60: previous_states = {task["task_id"]: task.get("state") for task in hydrated} 61: engine = SyncFieldDAG(hydrated) 62: self.dispatcher.engine = engine 63: self.solver.engine = engine 64: graph_events = [ 65: { 66: "task_id": task_id, 67: "old_state": previous_states[task_id], 68: "new_state": task["state"], 69: "depth": 0, 70: "reason": "Dependencies evaluated after graph reload", 71: } 72: for task_id, task in engine.tasks.items() 73: if previous_states.get(task_id) != task.get("state") 74: ] 75: engine.sync_to_supabase(graph_events, self.sb) 76: 77: def _claim(self) -> dict | None: 78: rows = self.sb.rpc("claim_next_task_command").execute().data or [] 79: return rows[0] if rows else None 80: 81: def _finish(self, command_id: str, result: dict) -> None: 82: self.sb.table("task_commands").update({ 83: "status": "succeeded", "result": self._json_safe(result), "error": None, 84: "completed_at": datetime.now().isoformat(), 85: }).eq("id", command_id).execute() 86: 87: @classmethod 88: def _json_safe(cls, value): 89: if isinstance(value, datetime): 90: return value.isoformat() 91: if isinstance(value, dict): 92: return {key: cls._json_safe(item) for key, item in value.items() if key != "_events"} 93: if isinstance(value, (list, tuple, set)): 94: return [cls._json_safe(item) for item in value] 95: if hasattr(value, "__dict__"): 96: return cls._json_safe(value.__dict__) 97: return value 98: 99: def _fail(self, command_id: str, error: Exception) -> None: 100: self.sb.table("task_commands").update({ 101: "status": "failed", "error": str(error)[:1000], 102: "completed_at": datetime.now().isoformat(), 103: }).eq("id", command_id).execute() 104: 105: def drain(self) -> None: 106: # One drainer at a time. Concurrent drains corrupt the shared engine. 107: with self._lock: 108: while True: 109: command = self._claim() 110: if not command: 111: return 112: try: 113: self._finish(command["id"], self.execute(command)) 114: except Exception as error: 115: self._fail(command["id"], error) 116: 117: def execute(self, command: dict) -> dict: 118: command_type = command["command_type"] 119: task_id = command.get("task_id") 120: project_id = command.get("project_id") 121: payload = command.get("payload") or {} 122: actor = command.get("requested_by") or "dashboard" 123: 124: if command_type == "task.create": 125: new_id = task_id or f"T_{uuid4().hex[:10].upper()}" 126: self.sb.table("tasks").insert({ 127: "id": new_id, 128: "project_id": project_id, 129: "task_name": payload.get("title") or "Untitled task", 130: "state": payload.get("state") or "LOCKED", 131: "dependencies": payload.get("dependencies") or [], 132: "assigned_to": self._valid_assignee(payload.get("assigneeId")), 133: "deadline": payload.get("deadline") or None, 134: "priority": payload.get("priority") or "medium", 135: "estimated_duration_hours": payload.get("estimatedDurationHours") or 2, 136: "required_skills": payload.get("requiredSkills") or [], 137: }).execute() 138: self.reload_graph() 139: return {"taskId": new_id} 140: 141: if command_type == "task.update": 142: if not task_id: 143: raise ValueError("taskId is required") 144: field_map = { 145: "title": "task_name", "deadline": "deadline", "priority": "priority", 146: "estimatedDurationHours": "estimated_duration_hours", 147: "dependencies": "dependencies", "requiredSkills": "required_skills", 148: } 149: update = {column: payload[key] for key, column in field_map.items() if key in payload} 150: if not update: 151: raise ValueError("No supported task fields supplied") 152: self.sb.table("tasks").update(update).eq("id", task_id).execute() 153: self.reload_graph() 154: return {"taskId": task_id, "updated": list(update)} 155: 156: if command_type == "task.delete": 157: if not task_id: 158: raise ValueError("taskId is required") 159: self.sb.table("tasks").delete().eq("id", task_id).execute() 160: self.reload_graph() 161: return {"taskId": task_id, "deleted": True} 162: 163: if command_type == "task.transition": 164: if not task_id: 165: raise ValueError("taskId is required") 166: self._ensure_engine_task(task_id) 167: target = str(payload.get("state") or "") 168: technician_id = payload.get("technicianId") or actor 169: if target == "ACTIVE": 170: return self.dispatcher.process_start_report(task_id, technician_id) 171: if target == "COMPLETE": 172: return self.dispatcher.process_completion_report(task_id, technician_id) 173: if target == "BLOCKED": 174: return self.dispatcher.process_failure_report( 175: task_id, payload.get("failureType") or "SITE_NOT_READY", technician_id 176: ) 177: events = self.dispatcher.engine.update_task_state( 178: task_id, target, f"Dashboard command by {actor}" 179: ) 180: self.dispatcher._sync(events) 181: return {"task_id": task_id, "events_triggered": len(events)} 182: 183: if command_type == "task.assign": 184: if not task_id: 185: raise ValueError("taskId is required") 186: self._ensure_engine_task(task_id) 187: technician_id = SupabaseEventBus: 9: """ 10: Coordinates real-time Postgres mutations with the running 11: FieldOpsDispatcher and SyncFieldDAG engine. 12: 13: Single-direction: Supabase → DAG Engine. 14: Writes back def handle_db_change(self, payload: dict) -> None: 22: # realtime>=2.x wraps the row as payload["data"]["record"] 23: record = payload.get("data", {}).get("record") or payload.get("new") 24: if not record: 25: return 26: 27: task_id = record.get("id") 28: db_state = record.get("state") 29: 30: if not task_id or not db_state: 31: return 32: 33: task = self.dispatcher.engine.tasks.get(task_id) 34: if not task: 35: return 36: 37: current_memory_state = task["state"] 38: if current_memory_state == db_state: 39: return 40: 41: print(f"[EventBus] External change: {task_id} {current_memory_state} → {db_state}") 42: 43: try: 44: events = self.dispatcher.engine.update_task_state( 45: task_id, 46: db_state, 47: f"SyncField Event Bus: Synchronized from Supabase" 48: ) 49: if events: 50: self.dispatcher.engine.sync_to_supabase(events, self.sb) 51: except Exception as e: 52: print(f"[EventBus] Error processing {task_id}: {e}") 53: 54: 55: class RealtimeEventBusListener: 56: """ 57: 62: 63: def __init__(self, bus: SupabaseEventBus, async_sb=None): 64: self.bus = bus 65: self.async_sb = async_sb 66: self.channel = None 67: 68: def _on_postgres_change(self, payload: dict) -> None: 69: self.bus.handle_db_change(payload) 70: 71: async def start(self) -> None: 72: self.channel = self.async_sb.channel("tasks_realtime_sync") 73: self.channel.on_postgres_changes( 74: "UPDATE", 75: callback=self._on_postgres_change, 76: schema="public", 77: table="tasks", 78: ) 79: await self.channel.subscribe() 80: print("[EventBus] Subscribed to Supabase Realtime on table 'tasks'") 81: 82: def start_sync(self) -> None: 83: asyncio.create_task(self._start_wrapped()) 84: 85: async def _start_wrapped(self) -> None: 86: try: 87: await self.start() 88: except Exception as e: 89: print(f"[EventBus] Subscription error: {e}") 90: 91: async def stop(self) -> None: 92: if self.channel: 93: await self.async_sb.remove_channel(self.channel) 94: self.channel = None 95: 33: } 1: import { createClient, type SupabaseClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js"; 2: 3: let browserClient: SupabaseClient | null = null; 4: let cachedToken: { value: string; expiresAt: number } | null = null; 5: 6: async function getRealtimeToken() { 7: if (cachedToken && cachedToken.expiresAt > Date.now() + 30_000) return cachedToken.value; 8: const response = await fetch("/api/operations/realtime-token", { cache: "no-store" }); 9: if (!response.ok) throw new Error("Unable to authenticate Realtime"); 10: const data = await response.json() as { token: string; expiresIn: number }; 11: cachedToken = { value: data.token, expiresAt: Date.now() + data.expiresIn * 1000 }; 12: return data.token; 13: } 14: 15: export function getSupabaseBrowser() { 16: if (browserClient) return browserClient; 17: const url = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL; 18: const anonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY; 19: if (!url || !anonKey) return null; 20: browserClient = createClient(url, anonKey, { 21: accessToken: getRealtimeToken, 22: auth: { persistSession: false, autoRefreshToken: false, detectSessionInUrl: false }, 23: }); 24: return browserClient; 25: } 1: import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"; 2: import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"; 3: 4: export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) { 5: return twMerge(clsx(inputs)); 6: } 7: 8: export function initials(name: string) { 9: return name.split(" ").map((part) => part[0]).join("").slice(0, 2).toUpperCase(); 10: } 1: # Imagine Hack — Strategic Overview 2: 3: ## Project Narrative (for Judges) 4: 5: **One-liner:** 6: > An AI-powered construction workforce manager that reads your Telegram chat, assigns workers automatically, and handles last-minute disruptions in real time. 7: 8: **Elevator pitch (30 seconds):** 9: > "Every morning, construction foremen spend hours juggling WhatsApp and Telegram messages — 'Ah Ming MC today', 'Site B needs two more tilers'. They manually figure out who's available, who has the right skills, and who's closest to the site. We automate that. Our Telegram bot listens to your existing chat, parses natural language messages using AI, assigns workers based on skill and availability, and when someone calls in sick — it instantly suggests the best replacement. The dashboard shows you real-time worker status, utilization rates, and CO2 saved from smarter routing. It's smarter workforce management for the construction industry." 10: 11: --- 12: 13: ## Strategic Recommendations 14: 15: ### 1. Demo Flow — The "Before/After" Story 16: 17: The most memorable demo structure follows a pain → solution → impact arc: 18: 19: | Phase | What happens on screen | Judge takeaway | 20: |-------|----------------------|----------------| 21: | **Before** | Show a cluttered Telegram chat with fragmented messages ("Ah Ming MC", "Site 2 need 2 more", "Anyone free?") | "This is chaos" | 22: | **System listens** | Telegram bot logs into the group, messages appear on the dashboard live with NLP-parsed entities highlighted (person, status, skill, site) | "It understands messy chat" | 23: | **Disruption** | A new message: "Kumar MC today" | Trigger event | 24: | **Auto-reassign** | System highlights Kumar's assignment as at-risk, finds the best replacement based on skill + availability + proximity, shows the recommendation | "It thinks ahead" | 25: | **Approve** | Foreman types /approve or it auto-assigns, dashboard updates live | "It closes the loop" | 26: | **Impact** | Dashboard shows metrics: utilization rate, disruptions handled, CO2 saved, overtime reduced | "It actually moves the needle" | 27: 28: **Tip for the demo:** Plant a real person to send Telegram messages live during the demo so the NLP parsing feels authentic. Don't pre-record everything. 29: 30: --- 31: 32: ### 2. Suggested Architecture 33: 34: ``` 35: Telegram Chat 36: | 37: v 38: [Telegram Bot] -- listens for messages 39: | 40: v 41: [LLM Parser] -- extracts: who, what skill, which site, what status 42: | 43: v 44: [Database] -- stores workers, skills, assignments, sites, attendance 45: | 46: v 47: [Assignment Engine] -- simple matcher (MVP) / optimizer (future) 48: | 49: v 50: [Dashboard] -- Next.js app with real-time updates 51: ``` 52: 53: **Key design principle:** Keep the Telegram bot and the web app loosely coupled via the database. The bot writes events to the DB; the dashboard reads them; the assignment engine is a service that can be swapped out. 54: 55: --- 56: 57: ### 3. Module Breakdown (4+ people) 58: 59: | Module | What it does | Who (recommended size) | 60: |--------|-------------|----------------------| 61: | **Telegram Bot (Python)** | Connect to Telegram, listen for new messages, download voice notes, forward to NLP pipeline | 1 person | 62: | **NLP / LLM Pipeline** | Parse natural language messages using LLM API, extract structured entities (worker name, status, skill, site, time) | 1 person | 63: | **Assignment Engine** | Match available workers to open shifts/ sites based on skills + workload; suggest replacements on disruption | 1 person | 64: | **Dashboard (Next.js)** | Real-time worker status board, live message feed, approval flow, sustainability metrics | 1 person | 65: | **Integration & Data** | Database schema, seeding demo data, connecting all modules, demo scripting | Shared / 1 person | 66: 67: With 5 people this maps nicely — one per module + one to glue it together. 68: 69: --- 70: 71: ### 4. Key Technical Decisions 72: 73: | Decision | Recommendation | Why | 74: |----------|---------------|-----| 75: | **LLM** | Use an API (GPT-4o mini or Claude Haiku) for NLP parsing | Lowest cost, best accuracy for messy multilingual construction chat | 76: | **Message format** | LLM returns JSON: `{"worker": "Kumar", "status": "mc", "site": "Site B", "skill": "tiler", "confidence": 0.95}` | Structured output makes downstream logic trivial | 77: | **Database** | PostgreSQL (already set up via Supabase) | Already implemented with Prisma schema | 78: | **Realtime** | Server-Sent Events (SSE) or polling from dashboard | WebSockets add complexity; SSE is simpler for demo | 79: | **Telegram library** | Telethon (already implemented) | Already works, just needs to forward messages to the NLP endpoint | 80: | **Assignment algo** | Score-based: skill_match * 0.5 + availability * 0.3 + proximity * 0.2 | Simple to implement, easy to explain to judges | 81: 82: --- 83: 84: ### 5. Sustainability Metrics for Track 3 85: 86: To satisfy the Track 3 requirements, tie every assignment decision to measurable impact: 87: 88: | Metric | How it's calculated | Demo hook | 89: |--------|-------------------|-----------| 90: | **Utilization rate** | Hours worked / hours available per worker | Show the "before" (low utilization from manual assignment) vs "after" | 91: | **Disruption response time** | Time from Telegram message to reassignment | Show 30 seconds → under 1 second | 92: | **Travel distance saved** | Estimated km reduced by assigning nearest available worker | Show cumulative CO2 saved | 93: | **Overtime %** | Hours over 8 / total hours | Show reduction from balanced workload distribution | 94: 95: --- 96: 97: ### 6. Risks and Mitigations 98: 99: | Risk | Mitigation | 100: |------|-----------| 101: | **LLM hallucinates worker names** | Validate extracted names against the database; if no match found, flag for manual review | 102: | **Real Telegram chat has no construction messages yet** | Seed a demo script that sends realistic messages on a timer during the demo | 103: | **NLP fails on Singlish/Manglish/Mandarin mix** | Include few-shot examples in the LLM prompt with the specific slang your target chat uses ("MC", "sakit", "tak boleh", "on the way") | 104: | **Dashboard feels empty without real data** | Build a seed script that populates 5-10 workers, 3 sites, and a history of assignments | 105: | **Time running out** | Cut the optimization engine entirely; focus on the Telegram -> NLP -> Dashboard -> Reassignment pipeline, which IS the core innovation | 106: 107: --- 108: 109: ## Immediate Next Steps 110: 111: 1. **Finalize team roles** — assign each person to one module from the breakdown above 112: 2. **Set up the LLM API key** — get an API key for GPT-4o mini or Claude, test parsing 5 sample messages 113: 3. **Extend the Telegram bot** — add an endpoint that forwards incoming messages to the NLP pipeline and stores structured results in the database 114: 4. **Define the database schema** — tables for workers, sites, skills, assignments, attendance_events, messages 115: 5. **Build the dashboard skeleton** — real-time message feed + worker status board 116: 6. **Test the live reassignment flow** — send "Kumar MC" via Telegram, see it appear on the dashboard + get a replacement suggestion 117: 7. **Polish the demo narrative** — rehearse the pain -> solution -> impact arc 1: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import SyncFieldDAG 2: from task_project_counts.get(target_name, 0) 89: 90: if total_b == 0: 91: guessed_task.guessed = True 92: guesses[target_name] = guessed_task 93: continue 94: 95: parents = dep_counts.get(target_name, {}) t.get("shift_start") or "08:00" 35: shift_end = t.get("shift_end") or "18:00" 36: sh, sm = (int(p) for p in shift_start.split(":")[:2]) 37: eh, em = (int(p) for p in shift_end.split(":")[:2]) 38: window = AvailabilityWindow( 39: start=today.replace(hour=sh, minute=sm), 40: end=today.replace(hour=eh, minute=em), 41: source="shift", 42: confirmed=True, 43: ) 44: schedules[t["id"]] = [] 52: engine = SyncFieldDAG(hydrate_task_rows(db_tasks)) 53: 54: tech_data = sb_client.table("technicians").select("*").execute().data or [] 55: tech_db = {t["id"]: t for t in tech_data} 56: schedules = _build_schedules(tech_data) 57: 58: solver = VRPSolver(engine, tech_db) 59: notifier = StubNotifier(sb_client) 60: dispatcher = FieldOpsDispatcher(engine, solver, notifier, sb_client=sb_client) 61: set_dispatcher(dispatcher) 62: 63: event_bus = SupabaseEventBus(dispatcher, sb_client) 64: async_sb = await get_supabase_async_client() 65: realtime_listener = RealtimeEventBusListener(event_bus, async_sb=async_sb) 66: realtime_listener.start_sync() 67: command_processor = CommandProcessor(dispatcher, solver, sb_client) 68: command_processor.reload_graph() 69: command_listener = CommandQueueListener(command_processor, async_sb) 70: command_listener.start_sync() 71: 72: client = TelegramClient( 73: session="bot_session", 74: api_id=config.API_ID, 75: Failure Taxonomy & Domain Configurations --- 6: FAILURE_CATEGORIES = { 7: "RESOURCE": ["MATERIAL_MISSING", "TOOL_DAMAGED", "TOOL_MISSING"], 8: "SITE": ["SITE_NOT_READY", "NO_POWER", "ACCESS_DENIED"], 9: "TECHNICAL": ["CONFIG_CONFLICT", "TEST_FAILED", "IP_CONFLICT"], 10: "QUALITY": ["BLUR", "WRONG_ANGLE", "DEFECTIVE_UNIT"], 11: } 12: 13: @dataclass 14: class FailurePolicy: 15: category: str 16: escalate_to_role: str 17: max_local_retries: int = 0 18: urgency_weight: float = 5.0 19: 20: 21: DEFAULT_POLICIES: dict[str, FailurePolicy] = { 22: "RESOURCE": FailurePolicy("RESOURCE", "procurement_lead", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=8.0), 23: "SITE": FailurePolicy("SITE", "project_manager", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=10.0), 24: "TECHNICAL": FailurePolicy("TECHNICAL", "network_specialist", max_local_retries=1, urgency_weight=15.0), 25: "QUALITY": FailurePolicy("QUALITY", "site_supervisor", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=5.0), 26: } 27: 28: 29: @dataclass 30: class AvailabilityWindow: 31: start: datetime 32: end: datetime 33: source: str 34: confirmed: bool = True 35: 36: 37: class TechnicianSchedule: 38: def __init__(self, windows: List[AvailabilityWindow]): 39: self.windows = sorted(windows, key=lambda w: w.start) 40: 41: def next_available_slot(self, after: datetime, duration: timedelta) -> tuple[Optional[datetime], str]: 42: for w in self.windows: 43: if w.end <= after: 44: continue 45: slot_start = max(after, w.start) 46: if slot_start + duration <= w.end: 47: confidence = "EXACT" if w.confirmed else "ESTIMATED" 48: return slot_start, confidence 49: return None, "UNKNOWN" 50: 51: def remove_window(self, start: datetime, end: datetime, source: str = "unavailable") -> None: 52: new_windows = [] 53: for w in self.windows: 54: if w.end <= start or w.start >= end: 55: new_windows.append(w) 56: else: 57: if w.start < start: 58: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(w.start, start, w.source, w.confirmed)) 59: if w.end > end: 60: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(end, w.end, w.source, w.confirmed)) 61: self.windows = sorted(new_windows, key=lambda w: w.start) 62: 63: def delay_from(self, when: datetime, by: timedelta, confirmed: bool = False) -> None: 64: new_windows = [] 65: for w in self.windows: 66: if w.start >= when: 67: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(w.start + by, w.end + by, w.source, confirmed)) 68: elif w.start < when < w.end: 69: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(w.start, when, w.source, w.confirmed)) 70: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(when + by, w.end + by, w.source, confirmed)) 71: else: 72: new_windows.append(w) 73: self.windows = sorted(new_windows, key=lambda w: w.start) 74: 75: # Define the explicit physical-world state space 76: VALID_STATES = { 77: "LOCKED", # Dependencies/preconditions not met 78: "READY", # Dependencies met, ready for dispatch 79: "ACTIVE", # Technician currently on-site working 80: "BLOCKED", # Started but halted by external issue (material/site) 81: "REGRESSED", # Was complete, but must be redone due to cascade/damage 82: "COMPLETE", # Work finished and verified 83: "FAILED", # Abandoned or cancelled 84: } 85: 86: # Strict transition matrix for direct manual/technician updates 87: VALID_MANUAL_TRANSITIONS = { 88: "LOCKED": {"READY"}, 89: "READY": {"ACTIVE", "LOCKED"}, 90: "ACTIVE": {"COMPLETE", "BLOCKED", "FAILED", "READY"}, 91: "BLOCKED": {"ACTIVE", "FAILED"}, 92: "COMPLETE": {"REGRESSED"}, 93: "REGRESSED": {"ACTIVE"}, 94: "FAILED": set(), 95: } 96: 97: MAX_CASCADE_DEPTH = 20 98: 99: @dataclass 100: class TaskPriority: 101: task_id: str 102: score: float 103: critical_path_len: int 104: days_to_deadline: Union[float, None] 105: hours_blocked: float 106: failure_category: Union[str, None] 107: granularity: str = "ESTIMATE" 108: data_sources_used: List[str] = None 109: 110: def __post_init__(self): 111: if self.data_sources_used is None: 112: self.data_sources_used = [] 113: 114: 115: class SyncFieldDAG: 116: def __init__(self, tasks_definition: List[dict]): 117: self.tasks: Dict[str, dict] = {t["task_id"]: t for t in tasks_definition} 118: self._precondition_hooks: Dict[str, List[Callable[[dict], bool]]] = {} 119: 120: for task in self.tasks.values(): 121: task.setdefault("state", "LOCKED") 122: task.setdefault("attempt_count", 0) 123: task.setdefault("failure_category", None) 124: task.setdefault("assigned_to", None) 125: task.setdefault("scheduled_start", None) 126: task.setdefault("created_at_epoch", datetime.now().timestamp()) 127: task.setdefault("resolution_eta", None) 128: task.setdefault("earliest_start", None) 129: task.setdefault("eta_confidence", "UNKNOWN") 130: 131: self.children_map: Dict[str, List[str]] = {t_id: [] for t_id in self.tasks} 132: for t_id, task in self.tasks.items(): 133: for dep in task.get("dependencies", []): 134: if dep in self.children_map: 135: self.children_map[dep].append(t_id) 136: 137: self._detect_cycles() 138: 139: self.evaluate_graph() 140: 141: def _detect_cycles(self) -> None: 142: visited: set = set() 143: rec_stack: set = set() 144: 145: def dfs(node: str) -> bool: 146: visited.add(node) 147: rec_stack.add(node) 148: for child in self.children_map.get(node, []): 149: if child not in visited: 150: if dfs(child): 151: return True 152: elif child in rec_stack: 153: return True 154: rec_stack.discard(node) 155: return False 156: 157: for t_id in self.tasks: 158: if t_id not in visited: 159: if dfs(t_id): 160: raise ValueError(f"Cycle detected in DAG — construction aborted") 161: 162: def get_descendants(self, start_node_id: str) -> Set[str]: 163: """Traverses downstream nodes using BFS to find all affected children.""" 164: descendants = set() 165: queue = [start_node_id] 166: while queue: 167: current = queue.pop(0) 168: for child in self.children_map.get(current, []): 169: if child not in descendants: 170: descendants.add(child) 171: queue.append(child) 172: return descendants 173: 174: def get_affected_technicians(self, blocked_task_id: str) -> List[dict]: 175: """Identifies every technician whose assigned work is downstream from a blocked node.""" 176: descendants = self.get_descendants(blocked_task_id) 177: affected = [] 178: 179: for t_id in descendants: 180: task = self.tasks[t_id] 181: if task.get("assigned_to"): 182: affected.append({ 183: "technician_id": task["assigned_to"], 184: "task_id": t_id, 185: "impact": "downstream_block", 186: "original_eta": task.get("scheduled_start") 187: }) 188: 189: direct_task = self.tasks.get(blocked_task_id) 190: if direct_task and direct_task.get("assigned_to"): 191: affected.append({ 192: "technician_id": direct_task["assigned_to"], 193: "task_id": blocked_task_id, 194: "impact": "direct_block", 195: "original_eta": direct_task.get("scheduled_start") 196: }) 197: 198: return affected 199: def compute_cascade_eta(self, blocked_task_id: str, schedules: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict: 200: """ 201: Propagates time impact downstream from a blocked node. 202: Confidence degrades: EXACT → ESTIMATED → UNKNOWN. 203: Optionally consults technician schedules for realistic start times. 204: """ 205: descendants = self.get_descendants(blocked_task_id) 206: order = [blocked_task_id] 207: queue = [blocked_task_id] 208: while queue: 209: current = queue.pop(0) 210: for child in self.children_map.get(current, []): 211: if child in descendants and child not in order: 212: order.append(child) 213: queue.append(child) 214: 215: results: dict = {} 216: 217: for t_id in order: 218: task = self.tasks[t_id] 219: deps = task.get("dependencies", []) 220: duration = timedelta(hours=task.get("estimated_duration_hours") or 2) 221: 222: if t_id == blocked_task_id: 223: if task.get("resolution_eta"): 224: results[t_id] = { 225: "earliest_start": task["resolution_eta"], 226: "earliest_finish": task["resolution_eta"] + duration, 227: "eta_confidence": task.get("eta_confidence", "EXACT"), 228: } 229: task["earliest_start"] = task["resolution_eta"] 230: task["eta_confidence"] = task.get("eta_confidence", "EXACT") 231: else: 232: results[t_id] = { 233: "earliest_start": None, 234: "earliest_finish": None, 235: "eta_confidence": "UNKNOWN", 236: "reason": "Blocked task has no resolution ETA", 237: } 238: task["earliest_start"] = None 239: task["eta_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN" 240: continue 241: 242: upstream_etas: list = [] 243: for dep in deps: 244: dep_task = self.tasks[dep] 245: if dep_task["state"] == "COMPLETE": 246: upstream_etas.append(("EXACT", datetime.min)) 247: elif dep_task.get("resolution_eta"): 248: dep_duration = dep_task.get("estimated_duration_hours") or 2 249: dep_finish = dep_task["resolution_eta"] + timedelta(hours=dep_duration) 250: upstream_etas.append(( 251: dep_task.get("eta_confidence", "ESTIMATED"), 252: dep_finish, 253: )) 254: else: 255: upstream_etas.append(("UNKNOWN", None)) 256: 257: any_unknown = any(conf == "UNKNOWN" for conf, _ in upstream_etas) if upstream_etas else False 258: any_estimated = any(conf == "ESTIMATED" for conf, _ in upstream_etas) if upstream_etas else False 259: 260: if any_unknown or not upstream_etas: 261: results[t_id] = { 262: "earliest_start": None, 263: "earliest_finish": None, 264: "eta_confidence": "UNKNOWN", 265: "reason": "Upstream resolution time not available" if any_unknown else "No dependency data", 266: } 267: task["earliest_start"] = None 268: task["eta_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN" 269: continue 270: 271: latest_upstream = max(eta for _, eta in upstream_etas if eta is not None) 272: assigned_tech = task.get("assigned_to") 273: 274: if schedules and assigned_tech and assigned_tech in schedules: 275: slot_start, slot_conf = schedules[assigned_tech].next_available_slot(latest_upstream, duration) 276: if slot_start is None: 277: results[t_id] = { 278: "earliest_start": None, 279: "earliest_finish": None, 280: "eta_confidence": "UNKNOWN", 281: "reason": f"{assigned_tech} has no available slot for {duration} from {latest_upstream}", 282: } 283: task["earliest_start"] = None 284: task["eta_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN" 285: continue 286: earliest_start = slot_start 287: effective_estimated = any_estimated or slot_conf == "ESTIMATED" 288: else: 289: earliest_start = latest_upstream 290: effective_estimated = any_estimated 291: 292: earliest_finish = earliest_start + duration 293: confidence = "ESTIMATED" if effective_estimated else "EXACT" 294: 295: results[t_id] = { 296: "earliest_start": earliest_start, 297: "earliest_finish": earliest_finish, 298: "eta_confidence": confidence, 299: } 300: task["earliest_start"] = earliest_start 301: task["eta_confidence"] = confidence 302: task["resolution_eta"] = earliest_start 303: 304: return results 305: 306: def register_precondition_hook(self, task_id: str, hook: Callable[[dict], bool]) -> None: 307: """Injects domain rules (e.g., inventory, site safety) into the generic engine.""" 308: if task_id not in self._precondition_hooks: 309: self._precondition_hooks[task_id] = [] 310: self._precondition_hooks[task_id].append(hook) 311: 312: def evaluate_graph(self, depth: int = 0, dirty: set = None) -> List[dict]: 313: """ 314: Maintains structural integrity by checking dependency states and hooks. 315: System cascades bypass the manual transition rules to enforce physical constraints. 316: Only READY tasks are re-locked — ACTIVE/BLOCKED/REGRESSED are protected. 317: """ 318: if depth >= MAX_CASCADE_DEPTH: 319: return [] 320: 321: candidates = dirty if dirty is not None else self.tasks.keys() 322: changes = [] 323: next_dirty = set() 324: 325: for t_id in candidates: 326: task = self.tasks[t_id] 327: current_state = task["state"] 328: 329: dependencies_met = all( 330: self.tasks[dep]["state"] == "COMPLETE" 331: for dep in task.get("dependencies", []) 332: ) 333: 334: hooks_passed = all( 335: hook(task) for hook in self._precondition_hooks.get(t_id, []) 336: ) 337: 338: if dependencies_met and hooks_passed: 339: if current_state == "LOCKED": 340: task["state"] = "READY" 341: changes.append({ 342: "task_id": t_id, 343: "old_state": "LOCKED", 344: "new_state": "READY", 345: "depth": depth, 346: "reason": "Dependencies and preconditions met" 347: }) 348: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 349: else: 350: if current_state == "READY": 351: task["state"] = "LOCKED" 352: changes.append({ 353: "task_id": t_id, 354: "old_state": "READY", 355: "new_state": "LOCKED", 356: "depth": depth, 357: "reason": "Prerequisites/preconditions no longer met" 358: }) 359: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 360: elif current_state == "BLOCKED": 361: task["state"] = "LOCKED" 362: if "blocked_since" in task: 363: del task["blocked_since"] 364: changes.append({ 365: "task_id": t_id, 366: "old_state": "BLOCKED", 367: "new_state": "LOCKED", 368: "depth": depth, 369: "reason": "Prerequisites no longer met — cleared block" 370: }) 371: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 372: elif current_state == "COMPLETE": 373: task["state"] = "REGRESSED" 374: changes.append({ 375: "task_id": t_id, 376: "old_state": "COMPLETE", 377: "new_state": "REGRESSED", 378: "depth": depth, 379: "reason": "Upstream dependency failed" 380: }) 381: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 382: 383: if changes: 384: changes.extend(self.evaluate_graph(depth + 1, dirty=next_dirty)) 385: 386: return changes 387: 388: def update_task_state(self, task_id: str, new_state: str, context: str = "") -> List[dict]: 389: """Updates a single task state manually while enforcing manual transition rules.""" 390: if task_id not in self.tasks: 391: raise ValueError(f"Task {task_id} not found.") 392: if new_state not in VALID_STATES: 393: raise ValueError(f"Invalid state: {new_state}") 394: 395: old_state = self.tasks[task_id]["state"] 396: if old_state == new_state: 397: return [] 398: 399: if new_state not in VALID_MANUAL_TRANSITIONS.get(old_state, set()): 400: raise ValueError(f"Illegal manual transition: {old_state} → {new_state} for task {task_id}") 401: 402: self.tasks[task_id]["state"] = new_state 403: 404: if new_state == "COMPLETE": 405: self.tasks[task_id]["attempt_count"] = 0 406: self.tasks[task_id]["failure_category"] = None 407: 408: if new_state == "BLOCKED": 409: self.tasks[task_id]["blocked_since"] = datetime.now() 410: elif old_state == "BLOCKED" and "blocked_since" in self.tasks[task_id]: 411: del self.tasks[task_id]["blocked_since"] 412: 413: print(f"
⚡ State Transition: [{task_id}] manually updated {old_state} ➞ {new_state} ({context})") 414: 415: events = [{"task_id": task_id, "old_state": old_state, "new_state": new_state, "depth": 0, "reason": context or "Manual operator update"}] 416: cascade_events = self.evaluate_graph(depth=0) 417: events.extend(cascade_events) 418: 419: return events 420: 421: def compute_priority(self, task_id: str) -> TaskPriority: 422: """Calculates a multi-factor priority score to guide allocation decisions.""" 423: task = self.tasks[task_id] 424: score = 0.0 425: data_sources: List[str] = [] 426: 427: # Factor 1: Critical path length 428: descendants = self.get_descendants(task_id) 429: critical_path_score = len(descendants) * 10.0 430: score += critical_path_score 431: data_sources.append("dag_topology") 432: 433: # Factor 2: Time pressure / Deadline proximity 434: deadline_score = 0.0 435: days_left = None 436: if task.get("deadline") is not None: 437: deadline = task["deadline"] 438: if isinstance(deadline, str): 439: deadline = datetime.fromisoformat(deadline.replace("Z", "+00:00")) 440: if deadline.tzinfo is None: 441: deadline = deadline.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) 442: days_left = (deadline - datetime.now(timezone.utc)).total_seconds() / 86400.0 443: deadline_score = max(0.0, (7.0 - days_left) * 5.0) 444: score += deadline_score 445: data_sources.append("deadline") 446: 447: # Factor 3: Resource contention (shared tooling) 448: contention_score = 8.0 if task.get("requires_shared_tool") else 0.0 449: score += contention_score 450: if task.get("requires_shared_tool"): 451: data_sources.append("inventory") 452: 453: # Factor 4: Blocker age 454: hours_blocked = 0.0 455: blocker_score = 0.0 456: if task["state"] == "BLOCKED" and "blocked_since" in task: 457: bs = task["blocked_since"] 458: if isinstance(bs, str): 459: bs = datetime.fromisoformat(bs.replace("Z", "+00:00")) 460: hours_blocked = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - bs).total_seconds() / 3600.0 461: blocker_score = hours_blocked * 2.0 462: score += blocker_score 463: data_sources.append("blocked_since") 464: 465: # Factor 5: Chronological tiebreaker 466: tiebreaker = 1.0 / (task.get("created_at_epoch", 1.0)) 467: score += tiebreaker 468: data_sources.append("created_at") 469: 470: # Factor 6: Failure category urgency weight 471: category = task.get("failure_category") 472: category_weight = DEFAULT_POLICIES[category].urgency_weight if category and category in DEFAULT_POLICIES else 0.0 473: score += category_weight 474: if category: 475: data_sources.append("failure_category") 476: 477: granularity = "DETAILED" if task.get("deadline") is not None and task.get("estimated_duration_hours") is not None else "ESTIMATE" 478: 479: return TaskPriority( 480: task_id=task_id, 481: score=round(score, 2), 482: critical_path_len=len(descendants), 483: days_to_deadline=round(days_left, 1) if days_left is not None else None, 484: hours_blocked=round(hours_blocked, 1), 485: failure_category=category, 486: granularity=granularity, 487: data_sources_used=data_sources, 488: ) 489: 490: def print_state(self): 491: print("-" * 80) 492: print(f"{'ID':<5} | {'Task Name':<38} | {'State':<12} | {'Priority Score'}") 493: print("-" * 80) 494: for t_id, task in self.tasks.items(): 495: p = self.compute_priority(t_id) 496: print(f"{t_id:<5} | {task['task_name']:<38} | {task['state']:<12} | {p.score} (Blocker hr: {p.hours_blocked})") 497: print("-" * 80) 498: 499: def sync_to_supabase(self, events: List[dict], event in seen_tasks.items(): 509: task = self.tasks[task_id] 510: earliest = task.get("earliest_start") 511: task_upserts.append({ 512: "id": task_id, 513: "task_name": task.get("task_name"), 514: "state": event["new_state"], 515: "failure_category": task.get("failure_category"), 516: "attempt_count": task.get("attempt_count", 0), 517: "earliest_start": earliest.isoformat() if earliest else None, 518: "eta_confidence": task.get("eta_confidence", "UNKNOWN"), 519: "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(), 520: }) 521: 522: event_inserts = [{ 523: "task_id": e["task_id"], 524: "old_state": e["old_state"], 525: "new_state": e["new_state"], 526: "reason": e["reason"], 527: "triggered_by": self._triggered_by(e), 528: "depth": e.get("depth", 0), 529: } for e in events] 530: 531: if task_upserts: 532: try: 533: sb_client.table("tasks").upsert(task_upserts).execute() 534: except Exception: 535: stripped = [] 536: for row in task_upserts: 537: stripped.append({k: v for k, v in row.items() 538: if k in ("id", "task_name", "state", 539: "failure_category", 540: "attempt_count", "updated_at")}) 541: sb_client.table("tasks").upsert(stripped).execute() 542: if event_inserts: 543: sb_client.table("task_events").insert(event_inserts).execute() 544: 545: @staticmethod 546: def _triggered_by(event: dict) -> str: 547: reason = event.get("reason", "") 548: if "Dependencies and preconditions met" in reason: 549: return "system" 550: if reason == "Manual operator update": 551: return "manual" 552: return "cascade" 553: 554: 555: class FieldOpsDomainRules: 556: def __init__(self, policies: dict[str, FailurePolicy] = None): 557: self.policies = policies or DEFAULT_POLICIES 558: 559: @staticmethod 560: def find_failure_category(failure_type: str) -> str: 561: for category, types in FAILURE_CATEGORIES.items(): 562: if failure_type in types: 563: return category 564: raise ValueError(f"Unknown failure type: {failure_type}") 565: 566: def handle_task_failure(self, engine: SyncFieldDAG, task_id: str, failure_type: str, technician_id: str) -> dict: 567: task = engine.tasks[task_id] 568: 569: if task["state"] == "FAILED": 570: return { 571: "action": "ALREADY_FAILED", 572: "assignee": technician_id, 573: "msg": f"Task {task_id} is already FAILED — ignoring late failure report." 574: } 575: 576: category = self.find_failure_category(failure_type) 577: policy = self.policies.get(category) 578: if not policy: 579: raise ValueError(f"No policy for failure category: {category}") 580: 581: task["failure_category"] = category 582: task["attempt_count"] += 1 583: attempt = task["attempt_count"] 584: 585: if attempt <= policy.max_local_retries: 586: events = engine.update_task_state(task_id, "BLOCKED", f"Local retry {attempt}/{policy.max_local_retries} for {failure_type}") 587: return { 588: "action": "RETRY_LOCAL", 589: "assignee": technician_id, 590: "msg": f"Technical issue ({failure_type}). Retrying task locally. "bg-blue-50 text-blue-700 ring-blue-600/15", 127: className, 128: )} 129: > 130: {children ?? key.replaceAll("_", " ")} 131: </span> 132: ); 133: } 134: 135: export function Progress({ 136: value, 137: className, 138: indicatorClassName, 139: }: { 140: value: number; 141: className?: string; 142: indicatorClassName?: string; 143: }) { 144: return ( 145: <div 146: className={cn("h-2 overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-slate-100", className)} 147: > 148: <div 149: className={cn( 150: "h-full rounded-full bg-orange-500 transition-all", 151: indicatorClassName, 152: )} 153: style={{ width: `${Math.max(0, Math.min(value, 100))}%` }} 154: /> 155: </div> 156: ); 176: }) { 177: const index = name.charCodeAt(0) % avatarColors.length; 178: return ( 179: <AvatarPrimitive.Root 180: title={name} 181: className={cn( 182: "inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center overflow-hidden rounded-full font-semibold ring-2 ring-white", 183: avatarColors[index], 184: size === "sm" && "size-7 text-[10px]", 185: size === "md" && "size-9 text-xs", 186: size === "lg" && "size-11 text-sm", 187: size === "xl" && "size-16 text-lg", 188: className, 189: )} 190: > 191: {src && ( 192: <AvatarPrimitive.Image 193: src={src} 194: alt={name} 195: className="size-full object-cover" 196: referrerPolicy="no-referrer" 197: /> 198: )} 199: <AvatarPrimitive.Fallback>{initials(name)}</AvatarPrimitive.Fallback> 200: </AvatarPrimitive.Root> 201: ); 335: const menuRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null); 336: const [coords, setCoords] = useState({ top: 0, left: 0, width: 0 }); 337: 338: useEffect(() => { 339: if (!open) return; 340: const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => { 341: if (ref.current && !ref.current.contains(e.target as Node) && !menuRef.current?.contains(e.target as Node)) 342: setOpen(false); "text-slate-700" : "text-slate-400", 370: className, 371: )} 372: > 373: <span className="flex-1 truncate">{label}</span> 374: {selected.length > 0 && ( 375: <span 376: role="button" 377: tabIndex={0} 378: onClick={(e) => { 379: e.stopPropagation(); 380: onChange([]); 381: }} 382: onKeyDown={(e) => { 383: if (e.key === "Enter" || e.key === " ") { 384: e.preventDefault(); 385: onChange([]); 386: } 387: }} 388: className="flex cursor-pointer items-center justify-center rounded p-0.5 text-slate-400 hover:bg-slate-100 hover:text-slate-600" 389: > 390: <IconX className="size-3.5" /> 391: </span> 392: )} 393: <svg 394: className="size-4 shrink-0 text-slate-400 transition-transform duration-200" 395: style={{ transform: open ? "rotate(180deg)" : "rotate(0deg)" }} 396: viewBox="0 0 24 24" 397: fill="none" 398: stroke="currentColor" 399: strokeWidth="2" 400: strokeLinecap="round" 401: strokeLinejoin="round" 402: > 403: <path d="m6 9 6 6 6-6" /> 404: </svg> 405: </button> 406: {open && 407: createPortal( 408: <div 409: ref={menuRef} 410: role="listbox" 411: onPointerDown={(e) => e.stopPropagation()} 412: className="pointer-events-auto z-[100] overflow-hidden rounded-xl border border-slate-200 bg-white shadow-lg" 413: style={{ 414: position: "fixed", 415: top: coords.top, 416: left: coords.left, 417: width: coords.width, 418: animation: "fade-in 0.12s ease-out", 419: }} 420: > 421: {options.map((opt) => { 422: const isSelected = selected.includes(opt.value); 423: return ( 424: <button 425: key={opt.value} 426: type="button" 427: role="option" 428: aria-selected={isSelected} 429: className="flex w-full items-center gap-3 px-3 py-2.5 text-left text-sm transition-colors duration-100 hover:bg-slate-50" 430: onClick={() => 431: onChange( 432: isSelected 433: ? selected.filter((v) => v !== opt.value) 434: : [...selected, opt.value], 435: ) 436: } 437: > 438: <div 439: className={cn( 440: "flex size-4 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded border transition-colors", 441: isSelected 442: ? "border-orange-500 bg-orange-500 text-white" 443: : "border-slate-300", 444: )} 445: > 446: {isSelected && ( 447: <svg 448: className="size-3" 449: viewBox="0 0 24 24" 450: fill="none" 451: stroke="currentColor" 452: strokeWidth="3" 453: strokeLinecap="round" 454: strokeLinejoin="round" 455: > 456: <path d="M20 6 9 17l-5-5" /> 457: </svg> 458: )} 459: </div> 460: <span 461: className={cn( 462: "flex-1", 463: isSelected 464: ? "font-medium text-slate-900" 465: : "text-slate-600", 466: )} 467: > 468: {opt.label} 469: </span> 470: </button> 471: ); 472: })} 473: </div>, 474: document.body, 475: )} 476: </div> 477: ); 478: } 479: 480: export function Select({ 481: className, 482: children, 483: value, 484: defaultValue, 485: onChange, 486: ...props 487: }: React.SelectHTMLAttributes<HTMLSelectElement>) { 488: const options = useMemo( 489: () => 490: React.Children.toArray(children).filter( 491: ( 492: c, 493: ): c is React.ReactElement< 494: React.OptionHTMLAttributes<HTMLOptionElement> 495: > => React.isValidElement(c) && c.type === "option", 496: ), 497: [children], 498: ); 532: })} 533: </SelectPrimitive.Viewport> 534: </SelectPrimitive.Content> 535: </SelectPrimitive.Portal> 536: </SelectPrimitive.Root> 537: ); 538: } 539: 540: export function Textarea( 541: props: React.TextareaHTMLAttributes<HTMLTextAreaElement>, 542: ) { 543: return ( 544: <textarea 545: {...props} 546: className={cn( 547: "min-h-24 w-full rounded-lg border border-slate-200 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm outline-none placeholder:text-slate-400 focus:border-orange-400 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-orange-100", 548: props.className, 549: )} 550: /> 551: ); 552: } 553: 554: export function Label({ 555: className, 556: ...props 557: }: React.LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>) { 558: return ( 559: <label 560: className={cn( 561: "mb-1.5 block text-xs font-semibold text-slate-700", 562: className, 563: )} 564: {...props} 565: /> 566: ); 567: } 568: 569: export function Separator({ className }: { className?: string }) { 570: return <div className={cn("h-px bg-slate-200", className)} />; 571: } 572: 573: export function Skeleton({ className }: { className?: string }) { 574: return ( 575: <div className={cn("animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-100", className)} /> 576: ); ( 597: <DialogPrimitive.Trigger asChild>{trigger}</DialogPrimitive.Trigger> 598: ) : null} 599: <DialogPrimitive.Portal> 600: <DialogPrimitive.Overlay className="fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-slate-950/40 backdrop-blur-[2px] data-[state=open]:animate-in" /> 601: <DialogPrimitive.Content className="fixed left-1/2 top-1/2 z-50 max-h-[90vh] w-[calc(100%-2rem)] max-w-2xl -translate-x-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 overflow-auto rounded-2xl border border-slate-200 bg-white p-6 shadow-2xl outline-none"> 602: <DialogPrimitive.Title className="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-950"> 603: {title} 604: </DialogPrimitive.Title> 605: {description && ( 606: <DialogPrimitive.Description className="mt-1 text-sm text-slate-500"> 607: {description} 608: </DialogPrimitive.Description> 609: )} 610: <div className="mt-5">{children}</div> 611: <DialogPrimitive.Close className="absolute right-4 top-4 rounded-md p-1.5 text-slate-400 hover:bg-slate-100 hover:text-slate-700"> 612: <IconX className="size-4" /> 613: </DialogPrimitive.Close> 614: </DialogPrimitive.Content> 615: </DialogPrimitive.Portal> 616: </DialogPrimitive.Root> 617: ); 618: } 619: 620: export function Tabs({ 621: tabs, 622: defaultValue, 623: className, 624: useHash, 625: }: { 626: tabs: { value: string; label: string; content: React.ReactNode }[]; 627: defaultValue?: string; 628: className?: string; 629: useHash?: boolean; 630: }) { 631: const [value, setValue] = React.useState(defaultValue ?? tabs[0]?.value); 632: 633: React.useEffect(() => { 634: if (!useHash) return; 635: const sync = () => { 636: const hash = window.location.hash.replace("#", ""); 637: if (hash && tabs.some((t) => t.value === hash)) setValue(hash); 638: }; 639: sync(); 640: window.addEventListener("hashchange", sync); 641: window.addEventListener("popstate", sync); 642: return () => { 643: window.removeEventListener("hashchange", sync); 644: window.removeEventListener("popstate", sync); 645: }; 646: }, [useHash, tabs]); 647: 648: const onValueChange = (v: string) => { 649: if (useHash) { 650: setValue(v); 651: window.history.replaceState(null, "", `#${v}`); 652: } 653: }; 654: 655: const list = ( 656: <TabsPrimitive.List className="flex w-full gap-1 overflow-x-auto border-b border-slate-200"> 657: {tabs.map((tab) => ( 658: <TabsPrimitive.Trigger 659: key={tab.value} 660: value={tab.value} 661: className="shrink-0 border-b-2 border-transparent px-4 py-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-500 outline-none transition-colors hover:text-slate-900 data-[state=active]:border-orange-500 data-[state=active]:text-slate-950" 662: > 663: {tab.label} 664: </TabsPrimitive.Trigger> 665: ))} 666: </TabsPrimitive.List> 667: ); 668: 669: const content = tabs.map((tab) => ( 670: <TabsPrimitive.Content 671: key={tab.value} 672: value={tab.value} 673: className="mt-5 outline-none" 674: > 675: {tab.content} 676: </TabsPrimitive.Content> 677: )); 678: 679: return useHash ? ( 680: <TabsPrimitive.Root 681: value={value} 682: onValueChange={onValueChange} 683: className={className} 684: > 685: {list} 686: {content} 687: </TabsPrimitive.Root> 688: ) : ( 689: <TabsPrimitive.Root 690: defaultValue={defaultValue ?? tabs[0]?.value} 691: className={className} 692: > 693: {list} 694: {content} 695: </TabsPrimitive.Root> 696: ); 697: } 698: 699: export function Table({ 700: children, 701: className, 702: }: { 703: children: React.ReactNode; 704: className?: string; 705: }) { 706: return ( 707: <div className="w-full overflow-x-auto"> 708: <table 709: className={cn("w-full min-w-[720px] text-left text-sm", className)} 710: > 711: {children} 712: </table> 713: </div> 714: ); 715: } 716: export function Th({ 717: children, 718: className, 719: }: { 720: children?: React.ReactNode; 721: className?: string; 722: }) { 723: return ( 724: <th 725: className={cn( 726: "border-b border-slate-200 bg-slate-50/70 px-4 py-3 text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-slate-500", 727: className, 728: )} 729: > 730: {children} 731: </th> 732: ); 733: } 734: export function Td({ 735: children, 736: className, 737: colSpan, 738: }: { 739: children: React.ReactNode; 740: className?: string; 741: colSpan?: number; 742: }) { 743: return ( 744: <td 745: colSpan={colSpan} 746: className={cn( 747: "border-b border-slate-100 px-4 py-3 text-slate-600", 748: className, 749: )} 750: > 751: {children} 752: </td> 753: ); 754: }
repomix.instruction:
<!-- BEGIN:nextjs-agent-rules --> # This is NOT the Next.js you know
This version has breaking changes — APIs, conventions, and file structure may all differ from your training data. Read the relevant guide in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing any code. Heed deprecation notices. <!-- END:nextjs-agent-rules -->
## Quick start
```sh npm run dev # http://localhost:3000 npm run build # production build npm run lint # ESLint v9 flat config (not `next lint`) npm start # serve production build ```
## Stack
- **Next.js 16** + **React 19** — read docs in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before coding - **TypeScript** — `@/` maps to `./src/*` - **Tailwind v4** — uses `@import "tailwindcss"` (not `@tailwind` directives) - **React Compiler** enabled in `next.config.ts` (needs `babel-plugin-react-compiler`)
## Auth
- **better-auth** with Google OAuth + Prisma adapter + PostgreSQL - Server: `src/lib/auth.ts` — client: `src/lib/auth-client.ts` - API route: `src/app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts` - Middleware: root-level `proxy.ts` (not `src/middleware.ts`) — protects `/dashboard`
## Prisma v7
- Config: `prisma.config.ts` (new v7 format, not the old `prisma` CLI default) - Client generated to `src/generated/prisma/client` (custom path, gitignored) - Run `npx prisma generate` before first build; run `npx prisma migrate dev` for schema changes - Requires `DATABASE_URL` env var (loaded via `dotenv` in `prisma.config.ts`)
## Environment
Required vars: `DATABASE_URL`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `BETTER_AUTH_URL` Required (Supabase): `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` (Python), `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (frontend)
**`.env` contains live credentials** (Telegram bot token, Google OAuth secrets) — never log or commit them.
## Workflow
- **Never push directly to `main`** — always create a PR from a feature branch.
## Caveats
- No test framework installed — skip any test commands - No CI pipeline or `.github/` directory - Python/Telegram bot artifacts present (`bot_session.session`, `__pycache__/`) but unrelated to the Next.js app
